100207.description_ja_long: 船形山の裾野に立つ円錐形の独立峰で「加美富士」と親しまれる標高553m。坂上田村麻呂の射た矢がこの山で尽き「矢喰」に転じたなど、名にまつわる伝説の多い信仰の山。
100207.description_ja_short: 加美富士と呼ばれる端正な独立峰。矢喰の伝説を残す。
100207.description_en_long: Yakuraisan is a conical solitary peak of 553 m at the foot of Funagatayama, affectionately called Kami-Fuji. A mountain of faith rich in legends about its name, including one that an arrow shot by Sakanoue no Tamuramaro fell spent on this mountain.
100207.description_en_short: A shapely solitary peak known as Kami-Fuji, keeping the legend of Tamuramaro's spent arrow.

100242.description_ja_long: 飯豊連峰北端の雄峰で標高1,636m。春、農具の朳を担ぐ人の形の雪形が現れ、麓の村々の農事暦とされたことが名の由来。頂稜は草原が広がり高山植物が彩る。
100242.description_ja_short: 朳を担ぐ人の雪形が名の由来。花咲く飯豊連峰北端の雄峰。
100242.description_en_long: Eburisashidake is a proud 1,636 m peak at the north end of the Iide Range. It is named for a spring snow patch shaped like a man shouldering an eburi farm tool, once a farming calendar for villages below. Grassland covers the summit ridge, colored by alpine flowers.
100242.description_en_short: Named for a snow patch shaped like an eburi bearer; a flowery peak at the north end of the Iide Range.
